questions.question: List the components of the image degradation model and briefly describe each one. (3 marks)
keyPoints: h(x,y): The Point Spread Function (PSF) representing blur.

questions.question: Explain the Wiener filter. Discuss why it is considered a superior approach to restoration compared to the simple inverse filter. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Definition: Wiener filter is a minimum mean square error estimator that provides an optimal trade-off between inverse filtering and noise smoothing.
It explicitly incorporates both the degradation function H(u,v) and the noise-to-signal power ratio (K).
Limitation of Inverse Filter: It amplifies noise catastrophically, especially where H(u,v) is zero or small (F̂ = G/H = F + N/H).
Advantage of Wiener: The filter term |H|²/(|H|² + K) prevents division by small values of H, thus suppressing noise amplification.
It provides a statistically optimal solution for restoring images degraded by blur and additive noise.
